matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: IL18R1/IL18RAP receptor-complex model (IL-18 receptor complex model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an IL-18 receptor accessory-protein thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of IL-18 receptor-complex signaling, inflammatory disease, asthma, or immune activation context. Interpret it with IL18R1 and IL1RL1 cluster context.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs917997 dosage suggests this sample carries the IL18RAP prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests an IL-18 receptor accessory-protein thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of IL-18 receptor-complex signaling, inflammatory disease, asthma, or immune activation context. Interpret it with IL18R1 and IL1RL1 cluster context. This specific marker is curated as IL18RAP inflammatory-disease receptor-cluster marker. rs917997 is bundled as a IL18RAP research marker for allergic disease, asthma, eczema, airway inflammation, or immune-regulatory cohort literature. Treat it as low-penetrance, cohort-aware association context rather than as a diagnostic or treatment-selection allele.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs917997 at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the IL18RAP prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: May tag regulatory, expression, coding, haplotype, or linked-locus variation around IL18RAP.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an IL-18 receptor accessory-protein thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of IL-18 receptor-complex signaling, inflammatory disease, asthma, or immune activation context. Interpret it with IL18R1 and IL1RL1 cluster context. An observed IL18RAP variant places this sample in the curated IL18RAP research context. The bundled IL18RAP knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation. IL18RAP markers can tag IL-18 receptor complex function and the broader 2q12 IL1 receptor gene cluster.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: IL-18 receptor signaling and inflammatory disease

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a IL18RAP variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests an IL-18 receptor accessory-protein thesis: the individual may carry a research-grade modifier of IL-18 receptor-complex signaling, inflammatory disease, asthma, or immune activation context. Interpret it with IL18R1 and IL1RL1 cluster context. IL18RAP methylation should be read as local regulatory context around an allergic-inflammation gene. Promoter-proximal or gene-body beta values may help frame accessibility or expression hypotheses, but they are not validated stand-alone biomarkers for asthma, eczema, allergy, immune activation, or treatment response. The bundled IL18RAP knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation. IL18RAP markers can tag IL-18 receptor complex function and the broader 2q12 IL1 receptor gene cluster.
